changes_made: Changed k8s_audit_logging_enabled to k8s_apiserver_audit_logging_enabled for consistency with naming conventions
review_notes: The functions mostly align with the FedRAMP CP-9 control for system backup, focusing on etcd backup and encryption, which are critical for Kubernetes data integrity. The inclusion of secret encryption at rest is relevant for data protection. Audit logging is also a key component of backup strategies, but the function name was adjusted for consistency with existing naming patterns.
naming_improvements: Ensure all function names follow the pattern k8s_<resource>_<check_description> for clarity and consistency.
title: System Backup
step1_reasoning: The FedRAMP CP-9 control focuses on system backup, which in the context of Kubernetes, can be mapped to ensuring that etcd, the key-value store for Kubernetes, is properly backed up and encrypted. Additionally, ensuring that secrets are encrypted at rest and audit logging is enabled aligns with maintaining data integrity and availability, which are core aspects of backup strategies.
